rationale: clauses: (1a) include design inputs when DHF present — KILLED by inverting the dhf guard 'if not dhf_dir.exists()'->'if dhf_dir.exists()' and by dropping 'result.requirements[di_id].extend(refs)'->pass; (1b) without record-first inputs legacy output unchanged — KILLED by making the '| Design inputs (DHF) |' summary line unconditional ('if result.design_inputs:'->'if True:'; assertions 'Design inputs (DHF)'/'DI-2' not in legacy output caught it); the stricter 'if not dhf_dir.exists()'->'if False:' probe SURVIVED but is a proven equivalent mutant — find_design_docs/design_inputs return empty on a nonexistent dhf/ and tagged only fills from declared inputs, so behavior is identical with or without the guard; (2) per-design-input test-tag coverage reported — KILLED by dropping 'result.tests[di_id].extend(refs)'->pass (DI-1 no longer 'tagged (1 file(s))'), by forcing status='tagged (1 file(s))' (DI-2 no longer UNTAGGED), and by suppressing the table row print; (3) untagged inputs listed as stories without coverage — KILLED by 'untested = req_ids - covered_ids'->'untested = set()'; (4) reflected in traceability score — KILLED by 'coverage = ...'->'coverage = 100' (assertion '- [ ] Coverage 50%' caught the vacuous score)
